《PRODUCE 101 JAPAN》是韓國CJ E&M與日本吉本興業共同策劃的實境節目，節目以《PRODUCE 101》作為藍本，以101名來自日本各地的練習生作為主角，在節目中同吃同住、完成各種任務，最後獲選的11名成員將會組成一個團體並於2020年正式出道。

導師

I saw Produce 101 live twice at Tokyo Dome Stadium with my friends which was marvelous and unforgettable. The first time I saw their concert we were late. I remember I said "look at the time. The concert will have begun" It was ten to nine. The concert would've started by then. It was a worthwhile concert that was compelling enough to see again. They've been strutting their stuff since they were kids. At the end of the last encore they bowed onstage soaking up the applause. They were given a thunderous ovation. Produce 101 and the dancers and acrobats who were invited to perform alongside them gave a superb performance. The choreography, the lighting and stage design and the glow sticks were magnificent and surpassed expectations. After the concert I dined in a ramen restaurant nearby. I remember the table was sticky maybe because the staff forgot to wipe it up so I asked them to wipe it up. Later I spilled a bowl of soup all over my shirt and trousers so I had to wipe up the spilt soup. I had a friendly chat with the lady sitting at the nearby table who told me once upon a time an entire village was wiped out by a super gigantic earthquake which was fascinating to hear. When I finished my bowl of ramen I wiped my mouth on my sleeve lol and a piece of tissue. There's an elegant cafe nearby of which the interior and exterior are artistically and exquisitely designed. Just the other day my friend Peter spilt his cappuccino all over his work and down his tie so he had to wipe up this mess. I enjoy drinking coffee and chatting with the baristas there so much that I occasionally make it worth their while. Produce 101 is very career-minded who has a worthwhile job and career. My friend Sarah who's also very career-oriented is planning on having them endorse her company's products but she'll have to make it financially worthwhile for them. i consider music performing a worthwhile career. Sarah's brother is a bassoonist. She explained to us regarding a piece by Haydn "this passage should be played lyrically rather than rhythmically. Otherwise it's a bit cheap." I saw lots of his pencil marks on the score such as cantabile, silence, no pause here, just separate, clean pedal, more melodic voicing, remain soft, stay soft, quiet, hear tenor, clearer, articulate, in time, silvery soft, gentler, take time....Regarding teaching he said "I prefer payment in blocks. My students pay monthly or for four lessons at a time. For this month it's ok to pay on a lesson-by-lesson basis because maybe you wanna see what it's like to have lessons. From next month onwards pay monthly. Students of all standards are welcome. I'm also an accompanist who accompanies instrumentalists, singers and other performers for exams, auditions and live performances. Some of my adult students started learning the bassoon when they were eighteen. Their fingers are much slower and less dextrous than say a ten year old boy. So start training your fingers when you're still very young so that you can get much faster fingers. It'll improve your finger speed, dexterity, strength and accuracy to a large extent." He asked me to get him some Clementi scores since I can get them on the cheap from a friend of mine. Yesterday I assigned five hours for practising Produce 101's songs on my saxophone, piccolo, flute, oboe, cor anglais (english horn), clarinet, E-flat clarinet, bass clarinet, bassoon and contrabassoon. I practised with my friends who played glockenspiel, xylophone, vibraphone, chimes, timpani (kettle-drum) and celesta, all of which have definite pitch. Adding these instruments makes the music more rhythmic. Saxophone, cor anglais and clarinet are transposing instruments. Saxophone, clarinet and bass clarinet are single reed instruments whereas oboe, cor anglais and bassoon are double reed instruments. Flute is a reedless instrument.
